title: BSA — Casino AML Program
text: Each casino with ≥$1M in annual gross revenue must implement an AML program with: internal controls, designated compliance officer, ongoing training, independent testing, customer identification + due diligence, SAR + CTR filing, recordkeeping. Risk-based EDD for high-risk customers (PEPs, high-volume, junket operators).

title: Currency Transaction Report — Casino
text: Casinos must file CTRC for cash-in OR cash-out transactions exceeding $10,000 in a gaming day per customer. Aggregate multiple transactions within a single gaming day. Filing within 15 days of transaction. Structuring (deliberately keeping individual transactions under $10K) is a separate violation reportable on SAR.

title: Suspicious Activity Report — Casino
text: Mandatory SAR for: transactions ≥ $5,000 if known/suspected of involving illegal activity; transactions designed to evade BSA; transactions with no apparent lawful purpose. Filing deadline: 30 days from initial detection (60 days if no suspect identified). Confidentiality — never disclose SAR filing to subject.

All US gaming jurisdictions maintain self-exclusion programs. Operators must: check every patron against state SE list at financial transaction + at high-value play; remove SE'd patrons from premises; void any winnings + return wagers; deny credit. Knowingly transacting with SE'd patron = regulatory violation + voided winnings.

section: UKGC Licence Conditions + Codes of Practice
title: UKGC LCCP — Social Responsibility Code
text: Licensees must: implement effective customer interaction policy + take action where indicators of harm appear; affordability checks for losses approaching financial vulnerability thresholds (£125/month review trigger; £500/month financial vulnerability check); deposit limits; reality checks; self-exclusion via GAMSTOP; no marketing to self-excluded.

section: Source of Funds — Enhanced Due Diligence
title: SOF / SOW evidence requirements
text: Operators must obtain + verify Source of Funds (SOF) + Source of Wealth (SOW) evidence for high-risk customers: customers triggering threshold review (typically £/€/$ 5K-10K cumulative deposits), PEPs, jurisdictions on FATF grey/black list, junket relationships. Acceptable evidence: payslips, bank statements, tax returns, asset valuations. Inability to evidence SOF = relationship termination.

title: Sports integrity — match-fixing
text: Suspicious betting patterns: late surge of large wagers on a specific outcome (vs market consensus); coordinated bets across multiple accounts; insider-information bets (athletes/officials/family); proposition bets matching pre-arranged outcomes. Operators must report to integrity monitoring (IBIA, SIGA, Sportradar) + suspend markets. Fixed-match co-conspirators face criminal liability + lifetime bans.

title: Underage gambling — verification
text: Minimum age for gambling: 21 in most US commercial casinos; 18 for state lotteries + parimutuel + some tribal; 18-21 by jurisdiction for online. Operators must verify age at registration + entry. Underage gambling = winnings void + returned wagers + regulatory violation + operator liability.

title: Chip walking + voucher fraud detection
text: Money-laundering typology: minimal play + cash-out (chip walking); buying chips with one funding source + redeeming with another (commingling); high-value voucher purchase without play; rapid voucher-to-cash conversions across multiple machines. Operators must monitor + file SAR for unexplained patterns.

title: Junket operator due diligence
text: Casinos using junket operators must conduct EDD on: junket operator (license, ownership, jurisdiction); each junket-introduced patron (source of funds, identity); commission arrangements (must not be tied to losses in violation of state law); cross-border movements (FinCEN CMIR for cash transport ≥$10K). Macau-style junket model carries elevated AML risk.
